scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented CIMIS Web API GET resource that the engine's declarative record extraction can express is covered — station metadata plus both zip-code reference lists (station zip codes and spatial/SCS zip codes), all served without an appKey. CIMIS exposes no write/mutation endpoints at all (a public read-only reference/observational data API), so capabilities.write stays false and this bundle ships no writes.json. /api/data (daily and hourly weather/ET observations) remains excluded as a genuine ENGINE_GAP (see docs.md Known limits) — unchanged from the prior review, re-confirmed against the live CIMIS REST reference during this pass.

excluded.reason: single-station-by-number filter of the same /api/station resource already covered whole by the stations stream; the engine's declarative read dialect has no per-record path-parameter filter primitive for a list stream, and a full station-list read already returns every station this endpoint would return individually
single-zip-code filter of the same /api/stationzipcode resource already covered whole by the station_zip_codes stream
single-zip-code filter of the same /api/spatialzipcode resource already covered whole by the spatial_zip_codes stream

records live nested at Data.Providers[].Records[] (an array within an array; the engine's RecordsAt only descends object keys via a dotted path, and cannot flatten across a dynamic number of provider entries), and each record's data-item fields (e.g. DayAirTmpAvg) are a DYNAMIC set of nested {Value,Qc,Unit} objects (driven by the requested dataItems config) each of which legacy flattens into 3 synthetic sibling fields (<Item>_Value/_Qc/_Unit) per key present -- there is no dotted-path wildcard or per-key dynamic-flatten primitive in computed_fields. Genuine ENGINE_GAP, tracked for a follow-up wave; covers both the daily and hourly legacy streams.
